Start by doing a domain name search to check if a domain name is available. Remember to also check it has not been trademarked.

3 things to remember before you buy domains

Keep it short

While there is no minimum length for domain registration, we recommend buying a domain name that’s under three words long. Longer website names are harder to read and won't stand out.

Less is more

Avoid hyphens, numbers, slang, and easily misspelled words in your domains. Complex elements make website names much harder to reach and remember.

Include your brand name

A great address for your website should include your brand or the target keywords for your niche. A search result with a keyword in your site URL will offer better brand recognition and increase your website’s traffic.

Frequently Asked Queries

Efficiently productivate reliable paradigms before ubiquitous models. Continually utilize frictionless expertise whereas tactical relationships. Still have questions? Contact us

What is a domain name?

A domain name, or simply domain, is similar to a home address in real life. It’s how people find your site online – they enter the domain in a web browser or search engine. NameBudget.net is a domain, just like Google.com and Facebook.com. Ideally, a domain should be registered under the same name as the brand it’s representing.

Why do I need to buy a domain name?

It is important to buy domain names to help potential visitors find your site. Without assigned website names, we would only be able to access them by entering their IP address, which is more difficult to remember. While performing a domain search to look for a good name, you can include keywords or brand names.

How to Search for a Perfect Website Name?

The best practices for buying domain names include the following: - Length. Keep it short. Two to three words is ideal. - Simple. Don’t use any lengthy or hard-to-spell words. - Keywords. Include a keyword from your niche. For example, if you sell coffee beans in Seattle, try Seattlecoffeebeans.com. - Avoid numbers. Numbers are hard to remember and make everything more complex. - Brand name. Buy a domain name that includes your brand for maximum recognition.

Once I register a domain, can I change it later?

No, once you register a domain, you cannot change it. However, you can buy another domain name and move your website there.

Protect your privacy – for free

When you register a domain, your personal information is usually available on public databases such as WHOIS. But it doesn’t have to be that way. At NameBudget, WHOIS privacy protection is included with every eligible domain registration. This keeps your personal information hidden from third parties.

24/7 live technical support

Our Customer Success team is here for you any time of the day. We've made our domain name registration process as easy as possible, but everyone needs a helping hand sometimes. Just contact us – we will be happy to assist.

Trusted domain registrar

NameBudget is an ICANN-accredited registrar and web host trusted by millions of people worldwide. We offer a comprehensive registration service with more than 100 domain extensions so that you can have plenty of choices.
